steps.title: Compute the result
steps.content: Simplify: $$f(8) = 5(64) = 320.$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice D is correct. It's given that the function $f$ is defined by $f\left(x\right)=5{x}^{2}$. Substituting $8$ for $x$ in $f\left(x\right)=5{x}^{2}$ yields $f\left(8\right)=5{\left(8\right)}^{2}$, which is equivalent to $f\left(8\right)=5\left(64\right)$, or $f\left(8\right)=320$. Therefore, the value of $f\left(8\right)$ is $320$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. This is the value of $f\left(8\right)$ if $f\left(x\right)=5x$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect. This is the value of $f\left(8\right)$ if $f\left(x\right)=5\left(x+2\right)$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is incorrect. This is the value of $f\left(8\right)$ if $f\left(x\right)=\left(5x\right)\left(2\right)$.
